gua juga nyelesein course dari anthropic atau claude buat tau gimana agentic workflow ini bisa dikasi sebuah job dan trial and error.

RT @pesaluyy: Within the 7 days of run, me and my agent bought my mom a laptop.

thank you @meridian_agent https://t.co/MO998vXjq1

sebenernya bikin agent itu dimulai dari cara otak lu mikir, bukan dari code-nya.

ada konsep namanya schema theory (Bartlett, Piaget). intinya otak manusia ga nyimpen fakta, dia nyimpen pola. waktu lu mau build agent, lu lagi mindahin pola pikir lu ke dalam mesin. dan lu gabisa mindahin sesuatu yang lu sendiri belum pahamin dengan jelas.

makanya sebelum nyuruh agentic coding nulis satu baris code, jawab ini dulu:

- sekarang kerjaan gua apa? bisa digantiin AI agent ngga?
- kalau iya, gimana workflow-nya step by step?
- data apa aja yang dibutuhin tiap step?
- kemungkinan masalah apa yang bakal muncul?
- output-nya apa? kapan agent tau dia udah "selesai"?
- kalau ada masalah, cara nyeleseinnya gimana?

oke gua kasih contoh nyata: content creator, analis, atau marketing.

tujuannya: bikin agent konten viral yang jalan otomatis.

langkah pertama, research agent. lu kasih agent akses ke TikTok, Reels, atau platform target lu. dia doomscroll, analisa konten yang perform tinggi di niche lu, catat pola hook-nya, format-nya, durasi, cara penyampaiannya. dia tau mana yang viral dan kenapa, bukan dari feeling tapi dari data.

langkah kedua, generation agent. dari pattern yang dikumpulin research agent, dia bikin prompt yang inject semua pola itu ke model generatif. hasilnya konten yang strukturnya udah proven works, bukan template kosong.

langkah ketiga, review agent. dia cek apakah output-nya feel natural atau masih keliatan AI slop. hook kuat di 3 detik pertama? tone-nya manusiawi? kalau belum, dia revise atau throw balik ke generation step.

langkah keempat, post dan analisa. agent track engagement, completion rate, share, save. semua data performance itu masuk balik ke research agent sebagai feedback loop. makin banyak yang dipost, makin pinter agent lu ngerti apa yang works di audience lu spesifik.

setiap step punya satu action, satu input, satu output. itu yang bikin agent bisa jalan tanpa bingung.

yang paling susah bukan teknologinya. yang paling susah adalah duduk dan beneran mikirin kerjaan lu sendiri sampai cukup detail untuk bisa lu delegate ke mesin.

yang bikin gua kaget dari komen post ini adalah

ternyata masih banyak orang yang ngira kalo langganan Claude/ChatGPT cuma buat chatbot doang?

padahal dengan claude/chatgpt kalian udah bisa bikin agent yang bakal gantiin kerjaan di depan laptop kalian

Another cool tool for CT deployed!!

I like that you can check out viral tweets from other accounts, and it’s also great for analyzing your content.

This tool made me reflect on virality, so here are some random thoughts and some advice for the future top content creators.

I've never been a viral account tbh, I often talk about more crypto-related/spiritual/educational type stuff that the X algo hates.

Based on my journey, virality is fun and great, but I would never make it the main goal when building a respectable brand for the long term.

Having a few bangers that you know can go viral is amazing, but I'd maintain a somewhat more genuine, valuable, educational, and serious approach if you want to achieve deal flow and real momentum in the space.

Many small/medium sized creators aim a lot for virality and often have to shitpost more, talk about politics or kinda sell their soul be relevant. You end up selling your personality and authenticity for views.

Don’t get me wrong, going viral works for certain things, but fast, cheap X engagement doesn’t equal user conversion or anything truly productive that serious web3 projects are looking for from creators.

If you want to work with the top protocols in web3, you must add top quality value to people's lives.

The truth is that people on social media don’t consciously interact with what’s most valuable to them or to society. They often chase cheap dopamine instead. Try to add the kind of value that empowers people and frees them from the constant need for dopamine. Educate them. Motivate them. Make them feel inspired, loved, and appreciated.

There are definitely some exceptions where aiming for virality makes sense:

- Launching shitcoins
- Memecoin trader
- Telegram gc owner
- Casinos/betting
- Gambling promos
- Prediction markets
- Only Fans
- Video clipping
- X monetization
- Politics/Geopolitics

If you are interested in any of the above, then sure, go for virality and shitpost as much as you can lmao. Your reputation and credibility will probably be harmed, but in these particular sectors, credibility is often inexistent.

For everyone else, have fun with viral tweets and some shitposting here and there, but don’t lose track of your purpose, your intention, your value, and your audience.

Virality eventually fades.

Credibility compounds.

Play the long game my friends.

Shoutout to @apcollective and @0xAbhiP. Really nice work.

Taxes? LMFAO

I have a bot running 24/7 buying and selling for a 0 target profit just to waste IRS manpower and money when I do my taxes.

I print and send the IRS all the documentation about my trades in paper form to stop them from automatizing the process with a computer program.

I mix blank pages, double prints, ghost prints, mark/write in them, etc just in case they have a way to scan and use some sort of recognition software to trivialize the work. That way they MUST look at thousands upon thousands of pages individually and see random schizo scribblings, swastikas, drawn dicks, etc while doing their job.

It's a whole pallet full of shit so I hire a guy to forklift and send the entire stack because I assume it's more troublesome for the IRS to deal with it that way.

All the documents are numbered but I pack everything "randomly" (wink wink) in case the timeline of the trades is important. I leave the first trades of the fiscal year at the bottom of the pallet AND at the bottom of each box so they now have to bend the knee to deal with boxes before they even start looking at the numbers.

My normal trades are also mixed in so they can't just ignore everything. I pay a tax managing company to make sure everything is flawless as I assume whoever has to deal with the bullshit extra work is going to triple check everything to fuck me over as a form of revenge which unknowing increases their work load further.

This costs me thousands of dollars every year. I make sure to send the invoices of how much all the processes cost me wrapped in the pallet so that whoever is in charge of it knows I'm wasting the equivalent of three months of their waging doing asinine shit.
